name: The I.W.C
whyWereHere:
The I.W.C. believes OWL has a problem, and unfortunately for everyone else, they think they are the solution. Led by loudmouth critic Will Komplane and enforced by heavy-handed muscle Buzz Kihl, the team operates like a walking comment section with authority issues. They claim they are here to “clean up” wrestling, correct bad behavior, and hold the roster to a higher standard—but really, they just want control.

Komplane does the talking, judging, and moral grandstanding, while Buzz makes sure nobody gets too comfortable disagreeing. Together, they turn every match into a lecture and every loss into somebody else’s fault. The I.W.C. came to OWL to censor chaos, punish imperfection, and prove that their version of wrestling is the only one that matters.

name: True North Elite
whyWereHere:
True North Elite was born inside Syrup City Wrestling Academy, where two very different kinds of privilege collided. Levi Martin came from money and wanted something that could finally challenge him. Connor Canada came from hockey, where his future as an all-world goalie was ruined by a temper he could never keep in the crease.

Together, they became a perfect problem: Levi brought polish, arrogance, and cold ambition, while Connor brought violence, anger, and the kind of mean streak that made opponents regret signing contracts. After running through the Canadian indies and collecting gold, True North Elite arrived in OWL looking for a bigger stage and better prey. They do not see themselves as hungry newcomers. They see themselves as superior athletes from the north, here to take what they believe already belongs to them.

name: B.T.T.I.T.W (Best Tag Team In The World)
whyWereHere:
BTTITW stands for Best Tag Team in the World, and yes, they picked the name before winning anything because humility was never part of the plan. Johnny Gargano and Zack Sabre Jr. formed out of tension, not friendship. They were two obsessive wrestling minds who kept clashing, countering, and testing each other until competition slowly turned into respect.

Their styles should not work together this well, but that is what makes them dangerous. Gargano brings speed, heart, counters, and big-match survival. Zack brings submissions, joint manipulation, arrogance, and the ability to make every opponent look like they skipped class. One fights with emotion, the other with calculation, and somehow they meet in the middle as a nightmare tag team.

BTTITW is not here to be liked. They are here to prove the name is not a joke.

name: BlackTide
whyWereHere: When Jeff Cobb and Brody King joined forces, OWL got hit by a natural disaster. Black Tide is power, violence, and destruction rolled into one team — two wrecking machines who don’t care about flash, approval, or playing nice. Cobb brings the explosive throws and impossible strength, while Brody brings the chaos and brutality that turns every fight into a nightmare. Together, they don’t just beat teams — they swallow them whole. Black Tide is here to wash over the division, drag the competition under, and leave nothing standing when the wave pulls back. If they’ve set their sights on the tag titles, it may already be too late.

name: Bounty Hunnaz
whyWereHere:
Bounty Hunnaz are not here to be fan favorites, role models, or tag-team purists. Gabe Kidd and Jacob Fatu formed around one simple idea: every roster has people who need to be dealt with, and they are more than willing to collect the price.

Kidd is the wild dog of the team—mouthy, reckless, and always ready to turn a match into a fight. Fatu is the monster behind him, a Samoan wrecking machine who makes sure nobody walks away once the hunt begins. Together, they feel less like a tag team and more like a warning notice. They do not chase glory the traditional way. They hunt targets, cash in damage, and leave teams wondering who put their name on the list.

name: Los Ingobernables: Plata o Plomo
whyWereHere:
Los Ingobernables: Plata o Plomo is not a normal tag team. It is an ultimatum. RUSH and Andrade operate with the same message for every team in OWL: accept their superiority, or get forced to understand it the hard way.

RUSH brings raw violence, pride, and the ability to turn any match into a fight no one is comfortable surviving. Andrade brings precision, arrogance, and the kind of ring IQ that makes every opening feel like a trap. Together, they are money and menace, luxury and brutality, style and punishment. They do not see the tag division as competition. They see it as territory.

The name says everything. Take the silver, take the beating, or get out of their way.

name: Double Dragon
whyWereHere:
Double Dragon is what happens when family, lucha libre, and competitive pride collide. Dragon Lee and Dralístico do not enter OWL as two random stars thrown together for convenience. They enter as brothers with shared history, shared blood, and very different ways of proving greatness.

Dragon Lee brings speed, precision, and controlled fire. Dralístico brings style, danger, and unpredictability. Together, they create a team that can outpace, outfly, and outthink almost anyone in the division. But underneath the masks, the moves, and the spectacle is something more personal: they are fighting to show that their family’s legacy is not finished being written.

Double Dragon is not here to simply compete. They are here to make the tag division feel the heat.
